analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet expresses national mourning in China for COVID-19 martyrs and victims, offers condolences, and conveys confidence that ongoing challenges will be overcome through solidarity and cooperation. Evaluation by narrative: CH1 (The West is immoral, hostile and decadent) – Not present; there is no mention or criticism of the West. CH2 (China is a benevolent power) – Partially aligned: the emphasis on solidarity and cooperation, while general, projects a cooperative, collectively oriented stance consistent with China's benevolent, multilateral image; however it is not explicitly about international roles. Still, the cooperative framing supports CH2 to a limited extent. CH3 (China has an epic history) – Not present; no historical framing or past adversities beyond current pandemic losses. CH4 (China has an appropriate political system and honorable values) – Some alignment: honoring martyrs, national mourning, and moral resolve suggest honorable values and social solidarity domestically, reflecting the system’s values. CH5 (The government of the CCP succeeds) – Not present; no claims of achievement or performance. CH6 (China is interesting culturally/naturally) – Not relevant. Final determination: CH2 (cooperative/solidarity framing) and CH4 (domestic values and honorable conduct) are supported.
